options.label: A
options.text: A magnet made from iron that is always magnetic

options.label: B
options.text: A magnet created by electricity flowing through a coil of wire

options.label: C
options.text: A permanent magnet found in nature

options.label: D
options.text: A device that converts magnetism into electricity

correct_answer: A magnet created by electricity flowing through a coil of wire
explanation: An electromagnet is made by wrapping wire around an iron core and passing electric current through it. The electric current creates a magnetic field around the coil. Unlike permanent magnets, electromagnets can be switched on and off by controlling the electric current.
